summary: App shell exposes the route container and the storage adapter that walkthrough mode mounts into and uses for any persisted state.

route_registration.name: registerRoute
route_registration.shape: (routeId: string, mountFn: (container: HTMLElement) => void, unmountFn?: () => void) => void
route_registration.purpose: Walkthrough mode registers itself under routeId='walkthrough' at boot; shell calls mountFn when the user navigates there.

storage_adapter.name: storage
storage_adapter.shape: { get(key: string): any|null, set(key: string, value: any): void, clear(prefix?: string): void }
storage_adapter.namespace_rule: All keys written by walkthrough mode MUST be prefixed with 'gto-trainer/v1/walkthrough/'. The adapter handles namespace prefixing internally — callers pass the suffix only.
storage_adapter.purpose: Walkthrough mode uses storage for any local state it persists (e.g., last-viewed hand id).

navigation.name: navigateHome
navigation.purpose: Walkthrough exposes a back-to-home affordance via this shell-provided function.

data_passed_not_logic: Shell passes a DOM container reference, three function references (storage methods), and a navigation callback. Shell does not implement any walkthrough logic; walkthrough does not implement any shell-level navigation or storage backend.

section-1_owns: storage adapter implementation and key namespacing
section-3_owns: any state stored via storage under its prefix

test_seam: An integration test mounts walkthrough into a test container by calling registerRoute with a stub container and verifying mountFn is invoked when navigateTo('walkthrough') is called.
